event: The Virgin Mary enthroned with the Christ Child, flanked by saints and the donor Sedano family
symbols: Virgin Mary enthroned in deep red robes — the Theotokos as Queen of Heaven, the Sedes Sapientiae (Seat of Wisdom)
Infant Jesus on Mary's lap — the Word made flesh, God incarnate held by human hands
angels attending and making music — the heavenly court ministering to the incarnate Lord in worship
Gothic architectural canopy — the church as sacred space within which the mystery of the Incarnation is encountered
donor figures kneeling in prayer on side panels — the faithful brought into Christ's presence through devotion and saintly intercession
ornate cross surmounting the triptych — the sign of redemption linking the tender Infancy to the Passion
description: A three-panel triptych with pointed Gothic arched frames. The central panel shows the Virgin Mary enthroned under an elaborate Gothic canopy, wearing a voluminous deep red robe. She holds the Infant Jesus on her lap. Two small angels attend nearby, one playing a musical instrument, while a young saint stands to the right. The left wing shows a male donor kneeling in prayer, presented by a standing saint, with a small child present. The right wing depicts a female donor kneeling, presented by a saint in a red cloak. Open landscapes with buildings are visible behind each group.
theological_description: Gerard David's triptych belongs to the tradition of Netherlandish devotional altarpieces uniting the heavenly court and human supplicants in a single devotional space. The central panel presents the Sacra Conversazione -- a holy gathering in the presence of the incarnate God. Mary's enthronement is a theological statement: she is the Theotokos, the God-Bearer, the living throne upon which the Creator rests in human infancy. As Isaiah foretold, 'the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel' (Isaiah 7:14). The Infant Christ fulfills the angel Gabriel's words: 'He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High' (Luke 1:32). The attending angels express the reality that even in his humiliation as an infant, Christ is worshipped by the heavenly host: 'Let all God's angels worship him' (Hebrews 1:6). The donor panels embody the communion of saints -- the Sedano family members kneel in prayer, presented by patron saints, expressing the belief that the faithful on earth, the saints in heaven, and the angels form one continuous Body of Christ united in worship across the boundary of death.

scripture_refs.reason: The virgin shall conceive and bear a son called Immanuel -- the Virgin holding the Christ Child fulfills this prophecy
The Annunciation promise fulfilled in the Infant Christ enthroned on Mary's lap
The Word became flesh and dwelt among us -- the Incarnation made visible in the divine Child held by his mother
Let all God's angels worship him -- angels attending the Christ Child in adoration
My soul magnifies the Lord -- Mary's Magnificat, her exaltation depicted in her enthronement
At your right hand stands the queen -- royal imagery applied to Mary enthroned in rich garments
You have come to innumerable angels in festal gathering, to the assembly of the firstborn -- the communion of saints united in worship across all three panels
